summary: Sprint 6 (Client Delight) full backend + frontend verification. Backend: 81/81 tests pass including 10 new Sprint 6 tests covering /api/search, PATCH /api/bookings/{id}, /api/bookings/conflicts, /api/dogs/{id}/stats, and dashboard upcoming_birthdays. Frontend: Global Search (button + Cmd/Ctrl+K + ESC), dog auto-open from search, lifetime stat pills, Gallery tab + Add Photos, Birthday banner all verified working with a seeded TEST_S6UI dog (3-day-out birthday + today daycare booking). Booking Edit modal opens with existing values and PATCH endpoint works. Two minor frontend issues found (see frontend_issues).

ui_bugs.issue: In EDIT mode, the primary submit button label still reads 'Create Booking' (instead of 'Save Changes'/'Update'). Title and subtitle correctly say 'Edit Booking · Update notes, kennel & times'. Confusing UX. Visible buttons captured during edit included only 'Create Booking', no Save/Update text.

Conflict warning panel [data-testid='booking-conflicts'] did NOT render after selecting TEST_S6UI client+dog with today's date while an existing booking for that dog+date exists. Backend GET /api/bookings/conflicts?dog_id=...&date_str=... correctly returns the conflict (verified by test_sprint6.TestBookingConflicts). Likely either a frontend filter excluding the new modal's case, or the dogId state changing in two ticks (clientId change resets dogId via clientDogs useMemo) prevented the debounced fetch from running with the final value. Worth a focused review of useEffect in AdminBookingModal.jsx lines 42-51 + clientDogs reset at lines 54-56.

issues: React hydration warning in console: 'In HTML, <span> cannot be a child of <option>' from AdminBookingModal client/dog <select> options — caused by ai-dev-tools wrapping each option's text in a tracked <span>. Not user-visible but pollutes console.

action_items: Change AdminBookingModal submit button label to 'Save Changes' / 'Update Booking' when isEdit is true.

Fix conflict-panel not rendering in NEW booking modal when client is switched then dog auto-resets (likely race in conflict useEffect). Confirm by manually opening +New Booking, picking the same dog as an existing booking and the same date — banner should appear.
Investigate ai-dev-tools <span> wrapper polluting <option> children — may be ignored if cosmetic only.
critical_code_review_comments: AdminBookingModal: button label not conditioned on isEdit; should switch text + maybe color/disable.

AdminBookingModal conflicts useEffect: when clientId changes, clientDogs useMemo resets dogId, triggering another render that may re-run conflict fetch with stale dogId. Consider combining into single effect that fires after both clientId & date stabilize.

GlobalSearch performs full-collection scan (find({}).to_list(2000) of clients AND dogs on every keystroke). For larger datasets this is O(N) per char — consider mongo $text index or $regex with text index later.

/api/search returns at most 10 results each but has no minimum query length on backend (line 1106 is `< 1` which allows 1-char queries against full collection scan). Consider raising to >=2 to reduce work.
seed_data_creation: Sprint 6 tests seed TEST_S6_-prefixed client/dog/booking per module fixture and tear them down. Frontend run additionally seeded TEST_S6UI_* (birthday +3 days, daycare today) and cleaned up after testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sprint 6 backend (search, PATCH bookings, conflicts, dog stats, upcoming_birthdays) is fully green. Frontend wiring verified: GlobalSearch, Cmd+K, ESC, dog auto-open edit modal, lifetime stat pills, Gallery tab + Add Photos, Birthday banner. Two issues to retest after E1 fix: (1) edit booking submit label, (2) conflict panel not rendering in NEW booking modal when picking an already-booked dog for today. ErrorBoundary UI was not triggered (component is mounted in App tree per code; impractical to crash app from outside). When seeding for UI tests use override_vaccines=true on bookings to avoid 422.
rca of the issue: Conflict-panel-not-rendering RCA hypothesis: In AdminBookingModal, opening +New Booking auto-selects clients[0] and then clientDogs (filtered list of dogs owned by that client) resets dogId to clientDogs[0]. Selecting a different client via the <select> triggers two state updates (clientId then dogId) — the conflicts useEffect debounces 200ms but the dogId change after clientDogs reset may not match the user's intended dog. The script picked from selects[1] BEFORE clientDogs had updated for the new clientId, so the dog value was rejected by the next clientDogs useEffect (line 54-56). Recommend: in AdminBookingModal, wait for client load THEN allow dog selection, or fire conflict fetch on (clientId, dogId, date) tuple.
